question.text: Under SEBI LODR Regulations 2015, who must be appointed as the Compliance Officer of a listed entity?

answer.text: A Chartered Accountant with at least 10 years of experience
Any senior management personnel nominated by the Board
A Cost and Management Accountant approved by SEBI

explanation.text: **The Logic:** SEBI LODR 2015 specifically mandates that the Compliance Officer of a listed entity must be a qualified Company Secretary. This ensures the person responsible for regulatory compliance has specialized knowledge of corporate laws and governance. **The Trap:** Candidates may assume any senior management personnel can be designated as Compliance Officer. While CAs and CMAs are important professionals, the LODR specifically requires a CS qualification for this role. **Cross-Exam Context:** NISM Series IX (Merchant Banking) covers listing compliance; Series II-A tests RTA awareness of who handles compliance at the issuer level. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** When processing corporate actions or investor complaints, always route regulatory queries to the listed entity's Compliance Officer (who must be a CS). This ensures proper regulatory handling and documentation.
